source.title: GMV Growth Decomposition and Trajectory Analysis (multiple sources)

extractions.claim: PDD's GMV growth is decelerating: from ~60% in 2023 to ~23% in 2024 to ~10-15% in 2025
extractions.evidence: In 2023, Pinduoduo's GMV was RMB 4.05 trillion. In 2024, analysts expected GMV to rise by +23% YoY, reaching CN¥4.8 trillion. 2025 expected growth rate of 10-15%. PDD's GMV is expected to grow at a CAGR of 12%, and revenue at a CAGR of 17%. Revenue CAGR outpacing GMV CAGR implies continued take rate expansion.

verification.verifier_notes: Growth deceleration is structural as domestic market matures. Domestic Pinduoduo is transitioning from hypergrowth to steady-state. International (Temu) was the growth engine in 2023-2024 but is now facing tariff headwinds.

extractions.claim: Growth decomposition: Domestic GMV growth driven by ARPU increase (not new users), International driven by new markets
extractions.evidence: Active buyers using Pinduoduo totaled 882 million in Q1 2022. Current estimates suggest 900M+. This implies domestic buyer growth is nearly exhausted (<3% growth). Average revenue per user continues to climb as users increase order frequency and purchase volume. Revenue growth decelerated from 59% in 2024 to ~10% in 2025 TTM.

verification.verifier_notes:
DECOMPOSITION:

Domestic (Pinduoduo):
- Users: ~900M, growing <3% (near saturation in China's 1.1B internet users)
- ARPU: ~$780, growing ~10-15% (trade-up + frequency increase)
- Domestic GMV growth: ~10-15% (ARPU-driven)

International (Temu):
- Users: 200-250M active, growing 20-30%
- ARPU: ~$300-350 (lower than domestic due to newer markets)
- Temu GMV growth: 15-40% (volatile due to tariffs, but structurally high)

Total GMV growth shifting from user-driven to ARPU-driven domestically, and market-expansion-driven internationally.

extractions.evidence: Following a triple-digit surge in early 2024, revenue has settled into a robust 10-15% YoY growth range in late 2025. Q3 2025 total revenues were RMB 108,276.5 million, up 9% YoY. Annual revenue for 2024 was $53.95 billion, a 54.46% increase YoY. TTM revenue through Q3 2025 was $57.327B, a 9.63% increase YoY.

verification.verifier_notes: Revenue growth deceleration is steep but stabilizing around 9-10% in 2025. This partly reflects: (1) lapping high Temu growth base, (2) domestic merchant subsidy programs reducing take rates, (3) US tariff impact on Temu. The Q4 2025 earnings (March 25) will reveal whether the 9-10% floor holds.
